terms.definition: The weighted degree to which members of a population share intelligible symbols, durable relational bonds, trusted procedures, and material interdependence
A modelled system whose boundary conditions exclude exogenous inputs except for explicitly declared shocks
A discontinuous jump in an order parameter once a control parameter crosses a critical threshold, borrowed from statistical physics
A systematic shift in the central tendency of a word’s denotative meaning over time
The proportion of adults engaged in long-horizon, kin-based commitments lasting more than ten years
Aggregate public confidence in legislative, judicial, and media bodies, typically measured via survey instruments
The linkage between production-output ratios and household security instruments such as wages and pensions
A macroscopic variable that captures the degree of order within a system, used to identify phase transitions
An external or slowly varying variable that drives changes in an order parameter within a system
A Shannon-information measure of unpredictability in word usage frequencies within a corpus
